items.name: 登川神社木彫掲額
items.description: 所有者：夕張神社。明治27年に出雲大社から登川神社に寄進されたもの。欅の材にトレースし「登川神社」と彫刻、文字を金箔で貼り付けた額。

en.name_en: Wooden Carved Hanging Tablet of Noborikawa Shrine
en.description_en: This is a tangible cultural property (fine art and crafts) consisting of a wooden carved hanging tablet of Noborikawa Shrine. It is a municipally designated cultural property of Yubari City, designated on 1985-05-21, located at Sumihatsu 7, Yubari City.

items.name: 東郷平八郎直筆の書
items.description: 所有者：夕張神社。東郷元帥が揮毫し夕張神社に寄贈したもの。

en.name_en: Calligraphy in the Hand of Togo Heihachiro
en.description_en: This is a tangible cultural property (fine art and crafts) consisting of a piece of calligraphy in the hand of Togo Heihachiro. It is a municipally designated cultural property of Yubari City, designated on 1985-05-21, located at Sumihatsu 7, Yubari City.

items.name: 欅の大杯
items.description: 所有者：夕張神社。直径62.5センチメートル、容量21.6リットル。松竹梅に鶴亀を描いた輪島塗。

en.description_en: This is a tangible cultural property (fine art and crafts) consisting of a large sake cup made of zelkova wood. It is a municipally designated cultural property of Yubari City, designated on 1985-05-21, located at Sumihatsu 7, Yubari City.

items.name: 採炭救国坑夫の像
items.description: 所有者：夕張市。中村直人氏と地元協力者により昭和19年に制作。コンクリート製、高さ3.63メートル。

en.name_en: Statue of a Coal Miner Saving the Nation
en.description_en: This is a tangible cultural property (fine art and crafts) consisting of a statue of a coal miner saving the nation. It is a municipally designated cultural property of Yubari City, designated on 1987-03-09, located at Takamatsu 6, Yubari City.

items.name: 労働のモニュメント
items.location: 夕張市南清水沢4丁目48番地12（オリジナル）、夕張市高松7番地（複製）
items.description: 所有者：夕張市。山内壮夫氏制作。縦1.90メートル、横1.10メートル、幅0.20メートル、18個のテラコッタ製ブロック。オリジナルは一般非公開、複製（ブロンズ製）は夕張市石炭博物館にて公開。

en.description_en: This is a tangible cultural property (fine art and crafts) consisting of a Monument to Labor. It is a municipally designated cultural property of Yubari City, designated on 1993-03-22, located at Minamishimizusawa 4-chome 48-12, Yubari City (original) and Takamatsu 7, Yubari City (replica).
